title: sagittaria trifolia subsp. edulis
intro: Sagittaria trifolia subsp. edulis, also known as wild potato or duck potato, is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the Alismataceae family. It is native to North America and has been used by indigenous communities for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of Sagittaria trifolia subsp. edulis include its potential to lower blood sugar levels, reduce inflammation, and support digestive health, making it a valuable addition to herbal remedies. The plant can be prepared in various ways, such as being dried and made into a tea or being cooked and eaten as a starchy root vegetable.
